result.chapter_summary: Chapter 2 argues that the recent national obsession with 'teacher quality' is a misguided attempt to blame instructors for the failures of structural reforms and flawed educational theories. Hirsch asserts that teacher effectiveness is contextual and that even excellent teachers are stymied by a content-incoherent curriculum and tests that do not align with classroom instruction.

claims.text: Teacher quality is not an innate characteristic or an inherent competence.
claims.source_passage: The effectiveness of a teacher is not some inherent competence, as the phrase teacher quality suggests.

claims.text: Statistical analysis of teacher effectiveness is misleading unless it is supported by a valid theory of underlying causes.
claims.source_passage: Statistical analysis is indispensable, but can be very misleading unless supported by a valid theory of the underlying causes of the results.

claims.text: In a coherent school environment, most teachers can become highly effective.
claims.source_passage: I have witnessed over and over that in a coherent school most teachers can become highly effective.

claims.text: The sudden national focus on 'teacher quality' is a result of reform fatigue and desperation following the failure of previous reforms.
claims.source_passage: I think it may be reform fatigue, possibly desperation. We are blaming teachers because of our disappointments with the results of our reforms.

claims.text: The state standards movement and No Child Left Behind (NCLB) have failed to significantly improve outcomes for high school students.
claims.source_passage: The state standards movement and the No Child Left Behind law have left high school students just about as far behind as they were before the law was instituted.

claims.text: Charter schools' overall results are not significantly better than those of regular public schools.
claims.source_passage: Charter schools, despite their laudable triumphs, are highly uneven in quality. Their overall results are not much better than those of regular schools.

claims.text: Educational reforms have failed because they have been primarily structural rather than focused on grade-by-grade curricular coherence.
claims.source_passage: But the most likely cause of disappointing results from the various reforms is that they have been primarily structural in character. They have not systematically grappled with the grade-by-grade specifics and coherence of the elementary school curriculum.

claims.text: The theory that reading consists of content-independent skills is mistaken and has allowed reformers to evade the issue of specific grade-by-grade content.
claims.source_passage: The underlying theory of the reforms (reflected in state reading standards) has been that schools are teaching skills that can be developed by any suitable content. That mistaken theory has allowed the problem of grade-by-grade content to be evaded.

claims.text: Current standardized reading tests are invalid because they do not test the specific content being taught in the classroom.
claims.source_passage: They apply the notions received in their training, and do what they are told to do by their administrators, under the ever-present threat of reading tests that do not actually test the content that is being taught.

claims.text: Teacher unions focus on contract stipulations and 'bread-and-butter' concessions because the classroom environment has become unproductive and punitive.
claims.source_passage: Under these extremely unfavorable conditions of work, it’s no wonder that teacher unions have focused on bread-and-butter concessions—and have pushed back against punitive but unproductive reforms.

claims.text: United States teacher preparation programs have failed to adequately prepare teachers for classroom management or subject-matter instruction for over fifty years.
claims.source_passage: It’s true that in the United States, there has been a deep problem with teacher preparation for more than half a century. We have a system that, according to teachers themselves, does not prepare them adequately for classroom management or the substance of what they must teach.

claims.text: School systems can decline even with highly qualified teachers if a substantive curriculum is replaced by fallacious skill-based ideas.
claims.source_passage: Yet, as I will illustrate with the example of France, even with a staff of well-trained, highly qualified, and well-educated teachers, the schools can suddenly decline when a substantive curriculum is abandoned, and fallacious ideas about skills begin to dominate.

claims.text: Teacher 'quality,' when measured by student outcomes, improves when teachers are provided with a specific and coherent curriculum.
claims.source_passage: If 'teacher quality' is to be judged by outcomes, their quality had suddenly risen [at Three Oaks School].

claims.text: An average teacher in a content-cumulative system is more effective than a superb teacher in a content-incoherent system.
claims.source_passage: Within the content-incoherent American primary school, it is impossible for a superb teacher to be as effective as a merely average teacher is in the content-cumulative Japanese elementary school.

claims.text: Successful teaching is more reliably produced by the coherence of the wider school system and teacher cooperation than by the individual brilliance of teachers.
claims.source_passage: The incident illustrated how good teaching can often depend more reliably on the coherence of the wider system, and the cooperation it brings, than on virtuoso performances.

claims.text: A specific and coherent curriculum allows the work of each teacher to build cumulatively on the work of their predecessors.
claims.source_passage: In a system with a specific and coherent curriculum, the work of each teacher builds on the work of teachers who came before.

claims.text: Curricular cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness provide a greater boost to student achievement than individual teacher brilliance in an incoherent system.
claims.source_passage: The three Cs—cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness—yield a bigger boost than the most brilliant efforts of teachers working individually against the odds within a topic-incoherent system.

claims.text: Systemic coherence improves teachers both individually and collectively.
claims.source_passage: A more coherent system makes teachers better individually and hugely better collectively.

claims.text: American teachers are victims of inadequate educational theories rather than being the source of intellectual failings.
claims.source_passage: American teachers (along with their students) are, in short, the tragic victims of inadequate theories. They are being blamed for intellectual failings that permeate the system within which they must work.

claims.text: The educational crisis in America is a problem of idea quality, not teacher quality.
claims.source_passage: The real problem is idea quality, not teacher quality.

claims.text: Teacher training has been compromised by theories of developmentalism, individualism, and the skills delusion.
claims.source_passage: The difficulty lies not with the inherent abilities of teachers but with the theories that have watered down their training, and created an intellectually chaotic school environment based on developmentalism, individualism, and the skills delusion.

claims.text: A specific curriculum would allow teachers to prepare more effectively and ensure they master necessary content.
claims.source_passage: Then teachers could prepare themselves more effectively, and teacher training could ensure that teacher candidates have mastered the content they will be responsible for teaching.

claims.text: Technological solutions like blended learning are based on a 'skills delusion' and have a history of failure in primary education.
claims.source_passage: Those who hope to find amelioration of the “teacher quality problem” through the use of computers and “blended learning” may be fostering yet another skills delusion. Technological fixes haven’t worked in the past.

claims.text: Primary education requires an empathetic personal connection that technology cannot provide.
claims.source_passage: Young students rely on an empathetic personal connection that not even our most advanced computer-adaptive programs can deliver.

claims.text: The common claim that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable for student learning is incorrect.
claims.source_passage: The assertion that “Teacher quality affects student learning more than any other school-based variable” is not footnoted. According to two summaries of research by Dr. Russ Whitehurst, a better curriculum can range from being slightly to dramatically more effective than a better teacher.

claims.text: Curriculum quality is more impactful than teacher quality because curriculum constitutes the actual content of learning.
claims.source_passage: That’s not surprising when you consider that the curriculum is what teachers teach and what students are supposed to learn.

claims.text: Value-Added Measures (VAMs) of teacher effectiveness are inherently unreliable and variable for language arts.
claims.source_passage: But, in fact, the results themselves cry out that something is amiss, since the value-added principle has exhibited far more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for math.

claims.text: Math tests are more accurate measures of teacher effectiveness than reading tests because math tests correlate highly with the taught curriculum.
claims.source_passage: In math, as I showed in chapter 1, there is a high correlation between what is supposed to be taught and what is actually tested, whereas that’s not true for the language arts curriculum and current reading tests.

claims.text: The value-added principle exhibits significantly more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for mathematics.
claims.source_passage: the value-added principle has exhibited far more uncertainty and variability for language arts than for math.

claims.text: Current reading tests fail to accurately gauge the specific value a teacher has added to a student's reading comprehension over a year.
claims.source_passage: Our current reading tests cannot in fact reliably and validly gauge the value the teacher has added.

claims.text: Reading test scores are a reflection of knowledge and vocabulary acquired from all life sources, not just school.
claims.source_passage: Scores on reading tests reflect knowledge and vocabulary gained from all sources.

claims.text: Advantaged students consistently build academic knowledge both inside and outside of school, whereas disadvantaged students rely almost exclusively on school for such knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Advantaged students are constantly building up academic knowledge from both inside and outside the school. Disadvantaged students gain their academic knowledge mainly inside school

claims.text: Current reading tests are curriculum-blind and therefore cannot measure how well a specific school curriculum has been taught.
claims.source_passage: Not being curriculum based, they cannot be an accurate means of testing how well the particular knowledge in the school curriculum has been imparted.

claims.text: The assumption that reading comprehension is a general, content-independent skill is a technical and theoretical error.
claims.source_passage: The implicit assumption that “general reading skill” is itself the content of the curriculum is a technical mistake and an incorrect assumption.

claims.text: Judges in legal proceedings should rule against the use of value-added measures for rating language arts teachers due to the falsity of their underlying assumptions.
claims.source_passage: Any judge in a lawsuit, properly alerted to the falsity of their assumptions, should rule against the fairness of value-added measures for rating language arts teachers.

claims.text: Test-based evaluations have caused the demoralization of millions of public school teachers.
claims.source_passage: Another evil consequence of test-based evaluations of language arts teachers has been the demoralization of millions of public school teachers.

claims.text: Teachers and administrators have been indoctrinated in individualistic, child-centered educational theories.
claims.source_passage: Like their administrators, they have been indoctrinated in individualistic, child-centered education.

claims.text: Teachers should reject the 'skills delusion' and demand a coherent, cumulative, multiyear content-based curriculum.
claims.source_passage: My plea to teachers—for the sake of their students, and themselves—is to rebel against the skills delusion; to insist on coherent and cumulative multiyear content

claims.text: Schoolteachers should not receive tenure protection in cases where they fail to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.
claims.source_passage: But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”; nor should there be for schoolteachers.

claims.text: Principals should prioritize spending on teacher development and the creation of collaborative planning environments over other expenditures.
claims.source_passage: If I were a principal in a primary school I’d spend my money on teachers, on their ongoing development, and on creating conditions in which the work of teachers in one grade supports the work of teachers in the next

claims.text: Tenure protections at universities were originally instituted to prevent the censorship of opinion.
claims.source_passage: Tenure protections at universities were instituted to avoid censorship of opinion.

claims.text: University tenure does not provide protection for a faculty member's failure to meet specified norms of performance or productivity.
claims.source_passage: But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”

claims.text: Primary school funding should prioritize teacher development and the creation of conditions that facilitate grade-to-grade pedagogical support.
claims.source_passage: If I were a principal in a primary school I’d spend my money on teachers, on their ongoing development, and on creating conditions in which the work of teachers in one grade supports the work of teachers in the next

claims.text: Teachers require dedicated time for collaborative planning and consultation with their colleagues.
claims.source_passage: and in which teachers would have time to consult and collaboratively plan.

claims.text: Educational success over a twelve-year period is the result of slow, consistent progress rather than intermittent bursts of individual teacher brilliance.
claims.source_passage: Its success depends on slow but sure progress, not bursts of brilliance—welcome as those are when talented teachers inspire a whole class.

claims.text: Collaboratively compiled handbooks and shared pedagogical strategies are more effective for solving student learning difficulties than individual teacher intuition.
claims.source_passage: The teacher shook her head: no, it wasn’t her brilliance that produced the result, and from her desk drawer she took out a handbook that teachers had cooperatively compiled. “Here it is,” she said. “It’s suggested as a good tack to try when you run into that situation.”

claims.text: The focus on teacher quality as the primary lever for reform is a symptom of 'reform fatigue' and desperation after previous structural strategies failed.
claims.source_passage: Only in the last few years has the teacher quality issue risen to the top. I think it may be reform fatigue, possibly desperation. We are blaming teachers because of our disappointments with the results of our reforms.

claims.text: When classroom environments become a 'purgatory' due to punitive reforms, teachers and unions logically shift their focus toward contract stipulations and bread-and-butter concessions.
claims.source_passage: When the classroom, which should be a daily reward, becomes a purgatory, one turns to contract stipulations.

claims.text: American teachers face the structural disadvantage of having to manage large discrepancies in student academic preparation, a problem Japanese teachers do not face due to their coherent system.
claims.source_passage: For one thing, the American teacher has to deal with big discrepancies in student academic preparation, while the Japanese teacher does not.

claims.text: Computers and 'blended learning' solutions are likely to be another 'skills delusion' because technological fixes have consistently failed in the past.
claims.source_passage: Those who hope to find amelioration of the 'teacher quality problem' through the use of computers and 'blended learning' may be fostering yet another skills delusion. Technological fixes haven’t worked in the past.

claims.text: Computer programs are inherently limited because they cannot provide the empathetic personal connection that young students rely on for primary education.
claims.source_passage: Young students rely on an empathetic personal connection that not even our most advanced computer-adaptive programs can deliver.

claims.text: The common assertion that teacher quality is the most important school-based variable is a non-footnoted claim that is contradicted by research from Dr. Russ Whitehurst.
claims.source_passage: The assertion that 'Teacher quality affects student learning more than any other school-based variable' is not footnoted. According to two summaries of research by Dr. Russ Whitehurst, a better curriculum can range from being slightly to dramatically more effective than a better teacher.

claims.text: Reading tests are at best 70 percent accurate at the individual level, making them statistically insufficient for high-stakes individual teacher evaluations.
claims.source_passage: Reading tests at best are 70 percent accurate at the individual level. The inherent uncertainty of the school-based contribution to a student’s reading scores between one year and another must reduce the validity of test inferences even more.

claims.text: Teachers should actively 'rebel' against the skills delusion and insist on a multiyear content-based curriculum for their own professional well-being.
claims.source_passage: My plea to teachers—for the sake of their students, and themselves—is to rebel against the skills delusion; to insist on coherent and cumulative multiyear content; then cooperate and consult.

claims.text: The inability of computers to accurately translate language, even after decades of development, serves as a theoretical proof of their limitations in primary education.
claims.source_passage: For example, after decades of work and billions spent, computers cannot accurately translate from one language to another. Probably they can’t even in theory. Such current limitations do not lend confidence that they can transform primary education.

evidence.description: The landmark 1983 report marking the start of the modern national education reform movement.
A study showing that charter school results are highly uneven and not significantly better than regular schools overall.
evidence.source_reference: Philip Gleason et al., The Impact of Charter Schools: Final Report (2010)

evidence.description: A report finding that education schools fail to teach teachers how to instruct reading effectively.
evidence.source_reference: Kate Walsh, Deborah Glaser, and Danielle Dunne Wilcox, What Education Schools Aren’t Teaching About Reading (2006)

evidence.description: The implementation of the Core Knowledge curriculum at Three Oaks School led to improved teacher morale and student test outcomes.
Comparison of effectiveness between teachers in content-incoherent US schools and content-cumulative Japanese schools.
Comparison between Japanese teachers who work within a coherent curriculum and American teachers who do not.
The failure of computers to accurately translate language despite decades of investment.
Research summaries showing that curriculum choice can be more effective than teacher quality.
SAT scores of undergraduate education majors ranking 25th out of 29 majors.
The mathematical formula used for Value-Added Measures (VAM) in teacher evaluation.
The Value-Added Measure (VAM) formula used to isolate teacher fixed effects from family and peer factors.
evidence.source_passage: τj is a teacher fixed effect that provides a measure of teacher value added for teacher j.

evidence.description: Individual-level reading tests show an average intertest correlation of only about 70 percent.
evidence.source_passage: Reading tests at best are 70 percent accurate at the individual level.

evidence.description: Teacher satisfaction in the US has declined from 62% in 2008 to 39% in 2012.
evidence.source_reference: The MetLife Survey of the American Teacher (2013)
evidence.source_passage: Teacher satisfaction has declined 23 percentage points since 2008, from 62% to 39% very satisfied

evidence.description: Anecdote of a Japanese fourth-grade math teacher using a specific analogy to help a struggling student, illustrating collaborative planning.
evidence.source_passage: One especially vivid story about collaboration in the Japanese elementary school was told to me directly by the late Professor Harold Stevenson... He had observed the event in a fourth-grade math class.

evidence.description: A report on faculty termination and disciplinary issues within university settings regarding the limits of tenure.
evidence.source_reference: Donna R. Euben, Faculty Termination & Disciplinary Issues, American Association of University Professors, 2004
evidence.source_passage: But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”

concepts.author_definition: A contextual effectiveness that arises from working within a coherent school system and curriculum, rather than an innate or permanent trait of an individual.
concepts.common_alternative: The inherent skill or competence of a teacher regardless of environment.
concepts.source_passage: The effectiveness of a teacher is not some inherent competence, as the phrase teacher quality suggests. Teacher effectiveness is contextual.

concepts.author_definition: The actual knowledge and content that students learn, which Hirsch argues is the true measure of educational success.
concepts.source_passage: Educational success is defined by what students learn—the received curriculum.

concepts.author_definition: Cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness; the curricular and organizational features that make teachers more effective collectively.
concepts.source_passage: The three Cs—cooperation, coherence, and cumulativeness—yield a bigger boost than the most brilliant efforts of teachers working individually

concepts.author_definition: The false belief that technology and computers can transform primary education by replacing the need for a knowledge-coherent curriculum.
concepts.source_passage: Those who hope to find amelioration of the “teacher quality problem” through the use of computers and “blended learning” may be fostering yet another skills delusion.

concepts.author_definition: A statistical method of evaluating teacher effectiveness based on student test score gains from one year to the next.
concepts.source_passage: The theory is that such a system will energize teachers, boost their performance, and bring highly qualified people into the profession.

concepts.author_definition: Standardized tests that measure general knowledge or skills without regard for the specific content taught in the classroom curriculum.
concepts.source_passage: Tests that are curriculum-blind cannot gauge how well a curriculum has been imparted.

concepts.author_definition: A mechanism intended to prevent censorship of opinion, which should not serve as a shield for poor performance or low productivity.
concepts.common_alternative: A job-security guarantee that protects teachers from being fired for almost any reason.
concepts.source_passage: Tenure protections at universities were instituted to avoid censorship of opinion. But even in universities there is no tenure protection for “failure to meet a specified norm of performance or productivity”

concepts.author_definition: The structural and collaborative alignment within a school system where curriculum and teacher efforts across grades support one another.
concepts.common_alternative: Administrative efficiency or standardized testing alignment.
concepts.source_passage: The incident illustrated how good teaching can often depend more reliably on the coherence of the wider system, and the cooperation it brings, than on virtuoso performances.

concepts.author_definition: A state of exhaustion or desperation among policymakers and the public after decades of educational reform strategies (like back-to-basics or NCLB) have failed to produce results.
An educational theory often coupled with 'individualism' that the author identifies as part of the 'skills delusion' hindering effective teacher training.
The human interaction between teacher and young student that the author argues is a non-negotiable requirement for primary education and cannot be replicated by technology.
Basic contractual stipulations regarding pay and working conditions that teacher unions focus on when the intellectual environment of the classroom becomes dysfunctional.

cases.name: France post-1989
cases.description: A case where schools declined suddenly after a substantive curriculum was abandoned and replaced by skill-based educational theories, despite having highly qualified teachers.
key_passages: even with a staff of well-trained, highly qualified, and well-educated teachers, the schools can suddenly decline when a substantive curriculum is abandoned, and fallacious ideas about skills begin to dominate.
contestable_aspects: Whether the decline was caused by curricular changes or other socio-economic factors.

cases.name: Three Oaks School, Fort Myers
cases.description: An early Core Knowledge school where teachers were initially intimidated by the content they had to teach but eventually saw success in morale and test scores.
key_passages: They were intimidated that first year by having to teach things they did not know themselves. The next year when I visited, they were enthusiastically explaining that they were learning these things along with the children. They started having a signal success in improved morale of students and teachers, and improved test outcomes.

cases.name: Japan
cases.description: Used as an example of a content-cumulative system where a specific and coherent curriculum allows average teachers to be highly effective because they build on the work of previous grades.
key_passages: it is impossible for a superb teacher to be as effective as a merely average teacher is in the content-cumulative Japanese elementary school.

cases.name: Japanese Curriculum System
cases.description: Japan is cited as a system where teachers do not face massive discrepancies in student preparation because they follow a specific and coherent curriculum.
key_passages: the Japanese teacher does not. In a system with a specific and coherent curriculum, the work of each teacher builds on the work of teachers who came before.

cases.name: VAM implementation in US Cities
cases.description: Major jurisdictions including Chicago, Washington DC, and New York City have implemented value-added measures for teacher evaluation and sanctions.
key_passages: Some jurisdictions, including Chicago, Washington, DC, and New York City, have instituted value-added measures (VAMs) of teacher effectiveness

cases.name: Japanese elementary school
cases.description: A Japanese fourth-grade classroom where a teacher used a specific analogy to solve a student's conceptual hurdle, cited as an example of the effectiveness of collaborative planning and common curriculum.
key_passages: One especially vivid story about collaboration in the Japanese elementary school was told to me directly by the late Professor Harold Stevenson... A student was having grave difficulty with a math problem and its concepts. After allowing the student to work on it for a short time, the teacher quietly made a surprising analogy

thinkers.name: Dr. Russ Whitehurst
thinkers.key_idea: Curriculum quality is a more significant variable in student achievement than teacher quality.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Whitehurst's research to debunk the 'scientific' claim that teacher quality is the single most important school-based variable.
key_passages: According to two summaries of research by Dr. Russ Whitehurst, a better curriculum can range from being slightly to dramatically more effective than a better teacher.

thinkers.name: Yehoshua Bar-Hillel
thinkers.key_idea: High-quality automatic machine translation is nonfeasible because computers cannot grasp unstated context.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Bar-Hillel to argue that computers have inherent limitations in understanding language, supporting his skepticism of technology-based primary education.
key_passages: Yehoshua Bar-Hillel famously argued it could not be solved... his argument, which is based on the insight that an unstated context is required for disambiguation.

thinkers.name: Harold Stevenson
thinkers.key_idea: Collaborative planning and classroom observation in Asian schools leads to superior pedagogical moments.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Stevenson's direct observations to support his argument for teacher collaboration and curriculum-based support.
key_passages: One especially vivid story about collaboration in the Japanese elementary school was told to me directly by the late Professor Harold Stevenson, who studied Asian schools.

thinkers.name: Eric Hanushek
thinkers.key_idea: The statistical use of teacher fixed effects (VAM) to measure quality.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch accepts the math but argues the underlying theory is invalid for language arts due to the nature of reading tests.
key_passages: Statistical analysis is indispensable, but can be very misleading unless supported by a valid theory of the underlying causes of the results.

actors.name: Randi Weingarten
actors.context: Head of the American Federation of Teachers cited for her willingness to see 'bad teachers' removed, used to show Hirsch isn't defending negligence.

actors.name: American Teacher Unions
actors.context: Described as being forced to focus on 'bread-and-butter' issues due to the 'purgatory' of the modern classroom.

actors.name: Governing Magazine
actors.context: Source of the 'new conventional wisdom' that blames teacher quality for student failings.

actors.name: Education Schools
actors.context: Accused of accepting under-qualified students and focusing on pedagogy/child development over subject-matter content.

actors.name: Public School Teachers
actors.context: Described as being unfairly scapegoated by VAM and 'indoctrinated' by education schools.

actors.name: American Federation of Teachers
actors.context: Cited regarding the removal of low-performing teachers from classrooms.

actors.name: Donna R. Euben
actors.context: Author of a report on university faculty termination for the AAUP.

actors.name: American Association of University Professors
actors.context: Cited regarding the standards for university tenure and faculty discipline.

actors.name: Harold Stevenson
actors.context: Expert on Asian schools whose anecdotal evidence supports the author's argument for systemic coherence.

objections_raised.objection: The flaw in education reform is not the theory, but poor implementation by teachers.
objections_raised.response: The failure lies in the structural character of reforms and the mistaken 'skills' theory, not in the teachers who are following instructions and using provided materials.

objections_raised.objection: Teacher quality affects student learning more than any other school-based variable.
objections_raised.response: Research by Russ Whitehurst shows that curriculum quality is often more impactful than teacher quality.

objections_raised.objection: Technology and blended learning can fix the teacher quality problem.
objections_raised.response: Technological fixes have failed in the past; they cannot replace the empathetic connection required in primary education or the need for a coherent curriculum.

objections_raised.objection: Reading tests can accurately measure the value added by a teacher during a single school year.
objections_raised.response: Tests cannot distinguish between knowledge gained in school versus knowledge gained outside of school, making them inherently unfair for evaluating teachers of low-SES students.

objections_raised.objection: The author's critique of teacher evaluations means they believe no teacher should ever be fired.
objections_raised.response: Individual teachers should be replaced if there is clear evidence of poor teaching, but VAM in language arts is not clear evidence.

objections_raised.objection: Tenure is an absolute protection that should prevent teachers from being fired for performance issues.
objections_raised.source: Implicitly attributed to defenders of current K-12 tenure practices
objections_raised.response: In university settings, tenure was designed to protect against censorship of opinion, not to protect against failure to meet performance norms; schoolteachers should be held to similar performance standards.

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: In math, as I showed in chapter 1, there is a high correlation between what is supposed to be taught and what is actually tested
In math, as I showed in chapter 1, there is a high correlation between what is supposed to be taught and what is actually tested
